about.description:
I'm a team lead and software engineer currently leading the migration of a national government platform — serving millions of users — to a microfrontend architecture, while mentoring interns and shipping high-impact features end to end.

With a systems engineering background and a master's in Artificial Intelligence, I combine hands-on engineering with a strategic, AI-aware approach to building scalable systems and resilient teams.

Outside of work, I recharge through hiking, reading, travel, and tinkering with new technology.

Scroll down for more on my experience, achievements, and projects.

responsibilities: Mentored & helped internship colleagues with the problems they encountered
Estimating the time needed to implement the requested features and assigning some of the tasks to other colleagues
Break down the documentation and client requests into tasks and sub-tasks.
Offering technical support for other colleagues
achievements: Led migration to a microfrontend architecture for a national government platform serving millions of users annually, defining the technical strategy and coordinating cross-functional teams through implementation and rollout. Doubled deployment frequency, enabled 15 teams to ship updates independently with zero cross-team blocking, reduced page load times by 62% via on-demand asset loading, and eliminated duplicated auth, token management, and UI logic by centralizing shared components in a dedicated shell application.

Replaced manual entry of owner details in the provisional vehicle registration process with a personalization app that captures the data digitally and prints it directly onto the authorization form, reducing data-entry errors by 90% and processing time by 50%.

Automated vehicle data entry for the national provisional vehicle registration system, increasing efficiency for millions of users annually. Independently implemented external validation against the Schengen National Information System (SINS) for new vehicle registrations, upgrading both front-end and back-end systems to support it and reducing manual verification time by 30%.

Boosted application performance by removing redundant database queries, reducing average response time by 20%, and reinforced system resilience using JMS, RabbitMQ, and dead-letter queues to manage user information and configurations effectively.
summary: Led migration to a microfrontend architecture for a government platform, doubling deployment frequency and enabling 15 teams to ship independently.
Reduced page load times by 62% and eliminated code duplication via a centralized shell application.
Digitized the provisional vehicle registration process, cutting data-entry errors by 90% and processing time by 50%.
Automated vehicle data entry and implemented SINS validation for new vehicle registrations.
Improved application performance and resilience using JMS, RabbitMQ, and dead-letter queues.

responsibilities: Provided support for code troubleshooting and debugging
Participated in daily stand-ups, sprint planning, and retrospectives
Mentored & helped internship colleagues with the problems they encountered
achievements: Contributed to the redesign of the Vehicle Registration app, reducing vehicle registration time by 50% and streamlining operator workflows.
Mentored an internship team of 4 interns in building an internal workspace-booking app, now actively used by 150 employees for seat management across the new office.
Designed and implemented a debug mode giving developers visibility into UI data processing, reducing average issue diagnosis time by 50%.
Built a caching mechanism with RxJS to eliminate repeated loading of large reference datasets (e.g., street dictionaries), reducing related load time by 80% and boosting overall UI performance.
Strengthened collaboration with the QA team by introducing new practices among developers, reducing average bug resolution time by 30% for shared libraries.
summary: Contributed to the redesign of the Vehicle Registration app, cutting registration time by 50%.
Mentored a 4-person internship team in building an internal workspace-booking app used by 150 employees.
Built a debug mode and an RxJS-based caching mechanism, improving diagnosis time and UI performance.
Improved QA collaboration practices, reducing bug resolution time by 30%.

education.faculty: Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers
education.description: Worked on diverse AI projects across multiple subfields, including Computer Vision, Deep Learning, Data Science, NLP, and Advanced AI Techniques, gaining experience handling real-world constraints using advanced model architectures. Projects ranged from predicting stock prices to pedestrian tracking and detection and autonomous drone flight.
achievements: Earned a merit scholarship for academic performance.
Engineered an autonomous drone system for the Introduction to Robotics course, maintaining stable real-time control within a 200ms processing budget on limited hardware.
Developed a novel pedestrian tracking and detection model, achieving 5% higher accuracy and 38% faster inference than existing approaches on limited hardware.

education.faculty: Faculty of Automatic Control and Computers
education.description: Completed coursework spanning Programming Languages (C), Object-Oriented Programming (C++), Operating Systems, Advanced Computing Architectures, and Web App Development in Java, building numerous applications and developing as a developer along the way.
achievements: Earned a merit scholarship for academic performance.
Competed in multiple hackathons, placing 3rd out of 12 teams in the DevHacks Hackathon.
Completed a Game Dev bootcamp organized by Gameloft, building a game engine from scratch and learning core engine architecture.
